title: Datadog PHP Extension Uninstall
entry_point: main() — called at script start unless DD_TEST_EXECUTION env var is truthy
activity_flow: START
[main] Check env DD_TEST_EXECUTION → if truthy: EXIT early (test mode)
[main] Call parse_validate_user_options()
[parse_validate_user_options] Call parse_cli_arguments($argv)
[parse_cli_arguments] Strip script name from argv, parse long (--) and short (-) flags
[parse_cli_arguments] If bare token starts with 'DD': map env-var to INI key via map_env_to_ini()
[parse_cli_arguments] Accumulate cmd (default='install') and opts map; return {cmd, opts} or false on parse error
[parse_validate_user_options] If parse failed OR -h/--help present → print_help() + exit(0)
[parse_validate_user_options] Normalize options: OPT_UNINSTALL, OPT_PHP_BIN, OPT_PHP_INI, OPT_INSTALL_DIR, OPT_EXTENSION_DIR, OPT_ENABLE_APPSEC, OPT_ENABLE_PROFILING, OPT_INI_SETTING
[parse_validate_user_options] If --php-bin provided with no value → print_error_and_exit() + print_help()
[main] Switch on cmd: CMD_CONFIG_GET → cmd_config_get(), CMD_CONFIG_SET → cmd_config_set(), CMD_CONFIG_LIST → cmd_config_list()
[main] Default branch: if OPT_UNINSTALL flag is set → call uninstall($options), else call install($options)
--- UNINSTALL PATH ---
[uninstall] Call require_binaries_or_exit($options)
[require_binaries_or_exit] If OPT_PHP_BIN is empty → call pick_binaries_interactive() (interactive, reads STDIN)
[require_binaries_or_exit] If OPT_PHP_BIN = 'all' → search_php_binaries(), filter out shebang scripts
[require_binaries_or_exit] Else: for each --php-bin value call resolve_command_full_path(); if not found → print_error_and_exit()
[require_binaries_or_exit] If resulting binary list is empty → print_error_and_exit('At least one binary must be specified')
[uninstall] FOR EACH selected binary:
[uninstall] Call ini_values($fullPath) → returns PHP properties: extension_dir, ini scan dir (INI_SCANDIR), main ini (INI_MAIN)
[uninstall] Determine extensionDir: use OPT_EXTENSION_DIR if set, else PHP's EXTENSION_DIR
[uninstall] Build extensionDestinations list: ddtrace.so, datadog-profiling.so, ddappsec.so (with platform prefix/suffix)
[uninstall] Determine iniFilePaths:
BRANCH A — INI_SCANDIR exists: primary path = INI_SCANDIR/98-ddtrace.ini
Sub-branch: if path contains '/cli/conf.d' (Debian): also add apache2/conf.d and fpm/conf.d variants if dirs exist
BRANCH B — INI_SCANDIR absent AND INI_MAIN absent: print_error_and_exit('no scan directory and no configuration file loaded')
BRANCH B — INI_SCANDIR absent AND INI_MAIN present: iniFilePaths = [INI_MAIN]
[uninstall] FOR EACH iniFilePath:
[uninstall] If path is a symlink → resolve with readlink()
[uninstall] If file exists:
If it IS 98-ddtrace.ini: modify_ini_file() → regex comment ALL extension= lines
If it is NOT 98-ddtrace.ini: modify_ini_file() → regex comment only ddtrace/datadog-profiling/ddappsec extension= lines
Echo 'Disabled all [Datadog] modules in INI file ...'
[uninstall] FOR EACH extensionDestination:
[uninstall] If file exists AND unlink() fails → print_warning(); set $errors = true
[uninstall] If $errors: echo 'Uninstall completed with warnings', else echo 'Uninstall successful'
END (loop continues for next binary)

key_functions.main(): Entry point; dispatches to install or uninstall based on --uninstall flag
key_functions.parse_cli_arguments($argv): Tokenises argv; handles --long, -s, DD_ENV= styles; returns {cmd, opts} or false
key_functions.parse_validate_user_options(): Wraps parse_cli_arguments; validates, normalises options; exits on --help
key_functions.uninstall($options): Core uninstall: per-binary INI commenting + .so removal
key_functions.require_binaries_or_exit($options): Resolves PHP binary list; interactive fallback; exits if none found
key_functions.ini_values($binary): Runs 'php -i' to extract extension_dir, ini scan dir, main ini path
key_functions.modify_ini_file($iniFilePath, $callback): Reads file, applies regex callback, writes back
key_functions.execute_or_exit($exitMessage, $command): exec() wrapper; calls print_error_and_exit on non-zero return code
key_functions.pick_binaries_interactive($options, $php: Interactive STDIN-based binary picker; recursive on bad input
key_functions.search_php_binaries($prefix): Finds PHP binaries in standard PATH locations
key_functions.resolve_command_full_path($command): Resolves a named binary to its absolute path

decision_points: DD_TEST_EXECUTION truthy → early exit (no-op for tests)
parse failed OR --help → print help + exit(0)
--php-bin empty → interactive binary picker vs CLI-specified list
INI_SCANDIR present vs absent → different INI file strategy
No scan dir AND no main ini → error exit
INI file is symlink → resolve before edit
INI file is 98-ddtrace.ini vs foreign INI → scope of regex comment differs
unlink() fails → warning (not fatal); $errors flag set

key_constants.INI_SCANDIR: PHP ini scan directory key from ini_values()
key_constants.INI_MAIN: PHP main ini file key from ini_values()
key_constants.EXTENSION_DIR: PHP extension directory key from ini_values()

ini_comment_strategy: Regex: prepend '; ' to lines matching (zend_)?extension= (all lines in 98-ddtrace.ini; only ddtrace|datadog-profiling|ddappsec in foreign INIs). File is NOT deleted — preserves custom settings.
important_notes: execute_or_exit() is a generic shell wrapper used broadly (not specifically in uninstall path, but available). Uninstall is non-destructive to INI files — only comments out extension lines. Debian multi-SAPI handling (cli/apache2/fpm conf.d) is automatic. Interactive mode triggers when --php-bin is omitted.
